Test Name: Different business types produce different section structures
Steps:
1. Generate page for 'local-business' (plumber)
2. Generate page for 'saas' (project management tool)
3. Compare section sequences
Expected Result: Plumber page includes services/service-area/testimonials. SaaS page includes features/pricing/how-it-works. Section sequences are visibly different.

Test Name: Different business types produce different hero variants
Steps:
1. Generate page for photographer (local-business, bold theme)
2. Generate page for SaaS startup (saas, clean theme)
3. Compare hero section variant
Expected Result: Photographer hero uses 'split-image' or 'background-image'. SaaS hero uses 'centered'. Variants are different and appropriate for business type.

Test Name: Gallery section appears for visual businesses
Preconditions: gallery added to strategist allowed types
Steps:
1. Generate page for photography business
2. Generate page for construction contractor
3. Check section types in output
Expected Result: At least one of the generated pages includes a 'gallery' section type. Gallery renders correctly via renderer.

Test Name: Service-area section appears for local businesses
Preconditions: service-area added to strategist allowed types
Steps:
1. Generate page for local plumbing business with city info
2. Check section types in output
Expected Result: Output includes 'service-area' section. Section renders with area names and descriptions.

Test Name: About-team section can appear when relevant
Preconditions: about-team added to strategist allowed types
Steps:
1. Generate page for consulting business
2. Generate page for coaching business
3. Check section types
Expected Result: At least one output includes 'about-team' section. Section renders with team/story content.

Test Name: themeVariant affects variant selection
Preconditions: selectVariant() uses themeVariant from preference table
Steps:
1. Generate page for same business with themeVariant='bold'
2. Generate page for same business with themeVariant='premium'
3. Compare variants chosen
Expected Result: Bold page uses more assertive variants (offer-focused hero, dual CTA). Premium page uses more elegant variants (split-image hero, single-highlight testimonials). At least 2 section variants differ.

Test Name: Secondary color used in section backgrounds
Steps:
1. Generate page with brand { primaryColor, secondaryColor, accentColor }
2. Inspect section background colors in output
Expected Result: At least one section uses secondaryColor (or a tint of it) as background instead of hardcoded #f8f9fa or #ffffff.

Test Name: Accent color used in visual elements
Steps:
1. Generate page with distinct accentColor
2. Inspect rendered HTML for accent color usage
Expected Result: accentColor appears in at least one rendered element (highlight, border, badge background, icon background). Not limited to only primaryColor usage.

Test Name: Pages no longer always use first variant for every section
Steps:
1. Generate 3 pages for different business types
2. Extract variant field from each section
3. Check for non-default variants
Expected Result: At least 30% of sections across the 3 pages use a non-default variant (not the first in SECTION_VARIANTS list). Multiple different variants appear.

Test Name: Simple businesses can produce shorter pages
Preconditions: Section count relaxed to 5-12 in strategist prompt
Steps:
1. Generate page for simple local business (e.g., barber shop)
2. Count sections in output
Expected Result: Output has fewer than 8 sections (e.g., 5-7). Page still includes hero, at least 2 content sections, CTA, contact, and footer.

Test Name: Premium theme produces different spacing than bold
Steps:
1. Generate page with themeVariant='premium'
2. Generate page with themeVariant='bold'
3. Compare section padding values
Expected Result: Premium sections have larger padding (e.g., 100px 0). Bold sections have tighter padding (e.g., 64px 0). Values are visibly different.

Test Name: Premium/bold/playful themes produce visibly different treatment
Steps:
1. Generate pages with each theme variant (clean, bold, premium, playful)
2. Compare rendered HTML styling
Expected Result: Bold: larger headings, tighter spacing, smaller border-radius. Premium: more whitespace, refined borders, serif-compatible. Playful: rounded corners, lighter colors. Clean: minimal, standard.

Test Name: Variation is coherent and not random noise
Preconditions: selectVariant() uses preference table + deterministic tie-breaking
Steps:
1. Generate page for a specific business type and theme
2. Review all section variants and colors together
Expected Result: All variants on the page feel cohesive: no jarring layout mismatches between sections. Color rhythm follows a logical pattern. Page reads as intentionally designed, not randomly assembled.

Test Name: Same business regenerated twice produces same variant layout
Steps:
1. Generate page for business X with context Y
2. Generate page again for same business X with same context Y
3. Compare section variants
Expected Result: Both generations produce the same variant selections for all sections. Layout is identical (deterministic). Only AI-generated text content may differ slightly.

Test Name: Different projects produce different variant choices
Steps:
1. Generate page for plumber project A
2. Generate page for plumber project B (same business type/theme)
3. Compare section variants
Expected Result: At least some section variants differ between the two projects due to different projectId seeds. Not all sections are identical.

Test Name: Non-default hero variants render correctly
Preconditions: split-image and background-image hero variants enabled
Steps:
1. Generate page that selects 'split-image' hero variant
2. Generate page that selects 'background-image' hero variant
3. Inspect rendered HTML
Expected Result: split-image hero: two-column layout with text and image. background-image hero: full-width background image with text overlay. No broken HTML or missing elements.

Test Name: Non-default testimonials variant renders correctly
Steps:
1. Generate page selecting 'avatars' testimonials variant
2. Generate page selecting 'single-highlight' testimonials variant
3. Inspect rendered HTML
Expected Result: avatars: shows testimonials with avatar placeholders. single-highlight: shows one featured testimonial prominently. No rendering errors.

Test Name: Non-default services variant renders correctly
Steps:
1. Generate page selecting 'alternating-rows' services variant
2. Inspect rendered HTML
Expected Result: alternating-rows: services displayed in left-right alternating layout. image-cards: services displayed as cards with image slots. No rendering errors.

Test Name: No white-on-white or dark-on-dark contrast violations
Steps:
1. Generate pages with various brand color combinations
2. Inspect all sections for text/background contrast
Expected Result: Every section has readable text: light backgrounds get dark text, dark backgrounds get light text. No section where text color matches background color.

Test Name: CTA band variant matches business CTA strategy
Preconditions: selectVariant() checks businessContext for CTA info
Steps:
1. Generate page for WhatsApp-first business (has WhatsApp number in context)
2. Check CTA band variant
Expected Result: CTA band uses 'whatsapp-focused' variant when WhatsApp contact is available. Falls back to 'centered' when no specific CTA strategy detected.
